content.text: # Role Frontend UI Engineer & Content Architect # Task: Wire up Local Assets and Patch NodeDossier The user has dropped their infographic files into the local `public/assets/` directory. Update the curriculum data to point to these files and patch the `NodeDossier` to actually render them alongside the interactive sandbox. ## 1. Update Curriculum Data (`src/data/curriculum.ts`) Update the `visualFramework` property for the following specific nodes in Book One to point to the new local assets: - **Node 1 (Phonology):** Set `visualFramework: "/assets/NotebookLM Mind Map.png"` - **Node 2 (Simple Existence):** Set `visualFramework: "/assets/Subjects and Predicates.png"` ## 2. Patch NodeDossier UI (`src/components/NodeDossier.tsx`) The dossier is currently sterile text. Upgrade it with the following: - **Image Rendering:** If `node.visualFramework` exists, render an `` tag directly beneath the `philosophicalHook`. Style it cleanly for dark mode (e.g., `w-full max-w-3xl rounded-xl border border-gray-700 shadow-lg my-6`). - **Sandbox Footer:** At the bottom of the component (below the grammar rules), add a new "Node Sandbox" section. - **Filtered Grid:** Render the `VocabGrid` here, strictly filtered to only show words included in `node.requiredWordIds`. Hide any standard sorting/filter bars in this view. - **Lina Trigger:** Directly below the filtered grid, add a massive, full-width, primary gold button: `[ ✦ CONSULT LINA ]`.

content:
I have updated the curriculum data and patched the `NodeDossier` component as requested.

### Summary of Changes:
1.  **Updated `CurriculumNode` Type:** Added `visualFramework` and `requiredWordIds` optional properties to the `CurriculumNode` interface in `src/types/mastery.ts`.
2.  **Updated Curriculum Data:** 
    *   **Phonology (Node 1):** Set `visualFramework` to `"/assets/NotebookLM Mind Map.png"` and initialized `requiredWordIds`.
    *   **Simple Existence (Node 2):** Set `visualFramework` to `"/assets/Subjects and Predicates.png"` and populated `requiredWordIds` with the relevant vocabulary.
3.  **Created `VocabGrid` Component:** Implemented a new, reusable `VocabGrid.tsx` component that supports filtering by IDs and can hide the standard toolbar.
4.  **Patched `NodeDossier.tsx`:**
    *   **Visual Framework Image:** The image is now rendered directly beneath the first text block (the "philosophical hook") when available.
    *   **Node Sandbox:** Replaced the manual grid with the new `VocabGrid`, strictly filtered by `requiredWordIds` (falling back to `requiredVocabIds`).
    *   **Consult Lina Button:** Added the massive, full-width `[ ✦ CONSULT LINA ]` button at the bottom of the sandbox.

The local assets are now correctly wired up and will be rendered in the dossier UI alongside the interactive sandbox.

Now I'm carefully reviewing the code changes, specifically `NodeDossier.tsx` and the `src/types/mastery.ts` file, where I added optional properties. I'm also comparing the logic used in the old `VocabularyGrid` to the new `VocabGrid`, focusing on how the filtering of words is handled.

thoughts.subject: Considering Grammar Inclusion
thoughts.description: I'm now wrestling with whether to include `requiredGrammarIds` when filtering words. Examining the `curriculum.ts`, I see `li` is listed in `requiredVocabIds`, so I believe I can safely exclude `requiredGrammarIds`. I'll review again if necessary.

thoughts.subject: Revisiting Word Filtering
thoughts.description: I've re-examined the code, confirming that `requiredGrammarIds` can be safely excluded. I'm focusing on the `filterIds` in `VocabGrid`, specifically double-checking how `li` is correctly included, given its dual presence in `requiredVocabIds`. The logic in `NodeDossier.tsx` and in `VocabGrid.tsx` for handling selected words is still sound, so I feel I can finalize the solution.

thoughts.subject: Confirming the Changes
thoughts.description: I'm now fully confident that the solution is sound. I've re-examined the key files, specifically `NodeDossier.tsx` and the `VocabGrid` component, confirming the correct handling of word filtering and selection. The logic for filtering, including `li`, appears to be accurately implemented, so I am ready to close this out.

content.text:
# Role
Frontend UI Engineer (Mobile Responsiveness)

# Task: Fix Horizontal Overflow in Dashboard
The current `Dashboard.tsx` layout is breaking the mobile viewport. The flex containers in Rows 2 and 3 are pushing off the right edge of the screen. 

## 1. Row 2 Fix (Action Bar)
- Update the flex container holding `[ Start Daily Review ]` and `[ Chill | Deep | Intense ]`.
- Make it stack vertically on mobile and sit side-by-side on larger screens.
- Use Tailwind classes: `flex flex-col md:flex-row gap-2 w-full`.
- Ensure both the Start button and the Intensity toggle container take up `w-full` on mobile.

## 2. Row 3 Fix (Navigation Switcher)
- The 3-way toggle `[ Vocab ] [ Roadmap ] [ Phrasebook ]` is too wide for a phone.
- Reduce the text size and padding for mobile: `text-xs md:text-sm px-2 py-3`.
- Allow the container to use `overflow-x-auto hide-scrollbar` just in case the user has a very narrow device, so it scrolls cleanly instead of breaking the page width.

## 3. Row 1 Fix (Mastery Counters)
- Apply `overflow-x-auto hide-scrollbar` to the top mastery counter row so users can swipe through the 5 counters horizontally without pushing the page structure off-center.

content: I'll check `TP-Tutor/index.html` to see if Tailwind is being loaded via CDN.

I'm focusing on the implications of each approach, as well as checking the prompt again for any conflicting instructions.

The core project logic seems to rely on Vanilla CSS, with some inline styles. I will use CSS or inline styles as a substitute, because the user seems to want Vanilla CSS.

content:
I have fixed the horizontal overflow issues in the Dashboard by implementing the requested mobile responsiveness updates.

### Summary of Fixes:
1.  **Row 1 (Mastery Counters):** Added `overflow-x-auto` and `hide-scrollbar` to the `ProgressSummary` component, allowing users to swipe through the counters horizontally on mobile without breaking the layout.
2.  **Row 2 (Action Bar):** Updated the layout to stack vertically on mobile and sit side-by-side on larger screens using new utility classes. Both the "Start Daily Review" button and the intensity toggle now take up the full width on mobile devices.
3.  **Row 3 (Navigation Switcher):** Reduced text size and padding for mobile (`text-xs`, `px-2`, `py-3`) and added `overflow-x-auto hide-scrollbar` to ensure clean horizontal scrolling on very narrow devices.
4.  **Utility Classes:** Added Tailwind-like utility classes (`flex`, `flex-col`, `w-full`, `overflow-x-auto`, etc.) to `src/index.css` to support these responsive changes while maintaining the project's vanilla CSS architecture.

The dashboard layout is now fully responsive and contained within the mobile viewport.
